data.content:
Worker B planning note for Claude Code approval support

Current findings
- Claude approval extraction is already partially covered. `createLineNormalizer("claude")` passes Claude stream-json objects through unchanged, and `extractApprovalRequest()` recognizes assistant `message.content[]` tool_use blocks named `AskUserQuestion`. The runtime emits those as `FOOLERY APPROVAL REQUIRED` banners; existing focused coverage is in `src/lib/__tests__/approval-request-visibility.test.ts` and `src/lib/__tests__/approval-request-runtime.test.ts`.
- Launch behavior is the missing half. `buildPromptModeArgs()` in `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts` always includes `--dangerously-skip-permissions` for Claude one-shot prompt sessions. The interactive launch paths duplicate the same hardcoded flag in `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-child-helpers.ts` and `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child-helpers.ts`.
- No source files were changed during this planning step. I created and bootstrapped `/Users/cartine/foolery/.worktrees/foolery-94da` on branch `codex/foolery-94da-claude-approvals` from `origin/main`, then ran `bun install --frozen-lockfile` successfully before inspection.

Recommended implementation plan
1. Add a single Claude permission-mode helper in `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts`, for example `shouldBypassClaudePermissions(agent)` plus `buildClaudePromptModeArgs()` and `buildClaudeInteractiveArgs()`. Default behavior must remain current autonomous mode: include `--dangerously-skip-permissions` unless the agent explicitly opts out.
2. Add a minimal explicit opt-out field to registered agent config, preferably `approvalMode?: "bypass" | "prompt"` or an equivalently clear name. Omitted/`bypass` keeps current behavior; `prompt` launches Claude without `--dangerously-skip-permissions` for approval-test agents. Update `src/lib/schemas.ts`, `src/lib/types.ts`, `src/lib/types-agent-target.ts`, `src/lib/settings-agent-targets.ts`, and `src/lib/agent-config-normalization.ts` only as needed so the field survives settings load/normalize/hydrate/target conversion. Update `docs/SETTINGS.md` with a short agent-field row and example snippet.
3. Replace the duplicated interactive Claude arg arrays in `buildAgentArgs()` and `buildSpawnArgs()` with the shared Claude interactive builder. Keep Codex/OpenCode/Gemini/Copilot paths untouched.
4. Strengthen Claude approval fixtures. Keep existing AskUserQuestion extraction, and add tests documenting the exact Claude stream-json shape Foolery supports: assistant event, tool_use block, `name: "AskUserQuestion"`, input questions/options, and tool id. Add a negative/edge fixture only if the implementation changes extraction behavior.
5. Add focused launch-arg tests. In `agent-adapter.test.ts`, assert default Claude prompt mode includes `--dangerously-skip-permissions` and `approvalMode: "prompt"` omits it while preserving `--output-format stream-json`, partial messages, verbose, and model args. Add tests for `buildAgentArgs()` and `buildSpawnArgs()` so both initial and take-loop interactive Claude launches cover default bypass and non-bypass modes.
6. Verification for the implementation step: run focused tests such as `bun run test src/lib/__tests__/agent-adapter.test.ts src/lib/__tests__/approval-request-visibility.test.ts src/lib/__tests__/approval-request-runtime.test.ts` plus any new helper tests, then run `bun run lint` and `bunx tsc --noEmit`. Run broader `bun run test` if schema/config normalization changes affect shared settings behavior.

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: small-to-medium. The code change is mostly arg construction and settings plumbing, but config normalization can silently drop new fields if missed.
- Main risk: naming the opt-out too generically and implying support for non-Claude CLIs. Keep docs explicit that the initial effect is Claude Code launch permissions.
- Secondary risk: if modern Claude emits a non-AskUserQuestion approval shape, extraction may still be incomplete. The implementation step should preserve AskUserQuestion coverage and add any observed real fixture if one is available from a non-bypass approval-test session.
- No child knots created: this knot is already a narrowly scoped Worker B subtask with clear ownership, so splitting further would add workflow overhead without reducing implementation risk.
